_doc: Canned responses extracted from the original investigation's raw/ CLI outputs (scrubbed). Substring dispatch, first match wins — specific rules precede generic ones.

rules._doc: Recorded verbatim. The session captured only the Error-level log query; the single row is the job's one Error entry — the CV Click ElementNotFoundException.
No unfiltered-logs raw was recorded; the Error-level set answers any logs variant — the exception row is the only diagnostic content.
Recorded verbatim: the Running -> Faulted transition set. Confirms a clean single execution — carries no diagnostic signal beyond jobs get.

Recorded verbatim. One RobotJob span whose Attributes carry the full UiPath.CV.ElementNotFoundException stack with the CvClickWithDescriptor.EndExecute faulting frame and the CVScope.OnFault propagation frame. No child CvClick/CVScope spans were emitted (no per-activity OpenTelemetry spans for this job), so the trace adds the verbatim .NET stack but no extra attribute-level disambiguation beyond jobs get/logs.
Alternate trace-retrieval form (platform traces command). Maps to the same recorded span payload so the investigation does not depend on which trace form the agent reaches for.

No --state Faulted raw was recorded in this session, so there is no Faulted-only fixture; substring dispatch routes `--state Faulted` variants here too, returning the full recorded list — the failing CV job (37e7a8bb...) is the most recent row.
Fallback for flag-order variations. The Shared folder's recorded job list is the correct answer for any jobs-list variant the agent tries; the most recent row is the faulted CV job.
docsai queries always passthrough — query strings vary between runs and canned responses go stale. Cached per-query within a run.

unmocked_default._doc: Permissive fallback: any uip command not matched by `rules` returns an empty array. Lets the agent explore beyond the recorded path without aborting.

expected_calls.description: Triage must resolve the Shared folder key (defb8e05-e36b-4c36-bf11-0b4d08ce6cd1) before listing jobs.
Agent must list jobs in folder Shared to identify the most recent (faulted) job — the CV process job 37e7a8bb...

Agent must fetch the failing job to read the UiPath.CV.ElementNotFoundException 'Element not found' fault from CvClickWithDescriptor ("CV Click - 'Button'") inside CVScope ("CV Screen Scope 'msedge.exe  Google'") in CV_ElementNotFound.xaml.

Trace retrieval surfaces the verbatim .NET stack confirming the faulting frame is CvClickWithDescriptor.EndExecute propagating through CVScope.OnFault — pinning the fault to the CV Click find, not scope setup or a downstream action.
OPTIONAL — Error-level log repeats the exception already visible in jobs get.
OPTIONAL — state transitions (Running -> Faulted) confirm a clean single execution.
